commit.message:
Strengthen moderator review queue: inline rejection panel, readiness signals, and feedback lifecycle fixes (#271)

* Initial plan

* feat: moderation review queue improvements

- Backend: explicit approval clears stale AdminNotes (null on PUBLISHED,
  trim on REJECTED) in ReviewEventAsync
- Backend: add ReviewEvent_Approve_ClearsStaleAdminNotes integration test
  verifying DB-level null after approval
- Backend: add ReviewEvent_Unauthenticated_ReturnsAuthError integration test
- Frontend AdminView: inline rejection panel with free-text notes, predefined
  category chips (Incomplete info, Weak description, Invalid URL, Wrong
  category, Past event, Duplicate), cancel/submit actions
- Frontend AdminView: readiness warnings (short description, missing venue/city/
  timezone/domain) shown as chips on PENDING_APPROVAL rows
- Frontend AdminView: attendance mode badge, submission age display
- Frontend AdminView: per-event loading state via eventReviewLoading ref
- Frontend: i18n keys added to en/sk/de for all new UI strings
- E2E mock-api: ReviewEvent handler now clears adminNotes on approval,
  matching backend semantics
- E2E: new tests for rejection with notes, category chips, approval clearing
  notes, readiness warnings; updated existing reject test for inline panel flow

commit.message:
feat: provider-agnostic PR URLs, Invoke-GitInit overhaul, and module improvements

- Replace gh CLI dependency with provider-agnostic -PullRequest support
  - Save-GitWorktree -PullRequest now opens the browser to the correct PR/MR
    creation page for GitHub, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket, GitLab, or any provider
  - New private Get-PullRequestUrl helper handles SSH + HTTPS remote formats
    including AzDo's ssh.dev.azure.com:v3/... SSH URL scheme
- Invoke-GitInit: replace -Private/-SkipRemote/gh with -RemoteUrl parameter
  - User creates remote on their provider first; no CLI tool required
  - Omitting -RemoteUrl creates a local-only bare repo
- Add Enter-GitWorktree (gitt goto) - navigate to worktree by branch or path
- Add Invoke-GitInit (gitt init) - bootstrap bare repo with optional remote
- Add Get-GitWorktreeStatus (gitt list) - color-coded worktree overview
- New-GitWorktree: Develop param set, -Author override, path-without-author
- Save-GitWorktree: -PullRequest switch (provider-agnostic browser open)
- Sync-GitBranches: fix porcelain parse (.Substring(3) not split on whitespace)
- DRY: extract Add-InitialWorktrees and Set-BareRepoGitConfig private helpers
- PS5.1 compatibility: lower PowerShellVersion, Open-WorktreeTerminal fallback
- Manifest: CmdletsToExport/VariablesToExport = @() instead of '*'
- 105 tests, 0 failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Git-Helpers

commit.message:
Generate Unicode category/script tables at runtime; update case fold to Unicode 17.0 (#126)

Replace ~2,000 lines of static Unicode category and script arrays with
runtime generation from JDK's Character.getType() and
Character.UnicodeScript.of(). This ensures categories and scripts always
match the running JDK's Unicode version.

Case folding (CASE_FOLD, TO_LOWER) remains static but is updated to
Unicode 17.0 (CaseFolding.txt), adding entries for Garay, Beria Erfe,
Cyrillic TJE, and several Latin pairs. Static fold tables can safely be
ahead of the JDK — entries for unrecognized code points are harmless
dead code, and Unicode's stability policy prevents reassignment.

Also fixes unicodeSpace() which incorrectly included U+001C-001F (file/
group/record/unit separators) — these are not in Unicode's White_Space
property. Filed as #125.

Adds UnicodeJdkConsistencyTest with canary tests that will fail when a
new JDK ships with a newer Unicode version that adds case pairs or
whitespace characters, signaling that the static tables need updating.

Fixes #107
Refs #125

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

for meaningful 2-state structure. 2000 bars (~83 days on H1) reliably
picks up calm/volatile regimes for assets like BTCUSD and XAUUSD.

commit.message:
Fix all PRs and security: repo-relative links, wiki navigation, workflow hardening, and action security fixes

* Add AI skill sync infrastructure with governance layer

- Canonical project spec (spec v1.0) with TrustSignal-specific rules
- Allowed/forbidden paths, decision rights, approval matrix
- Context drift controls with version discipline
- Model adapters for Claude, OpenAI, Gemini
- Agent policies (primary decision authority + executor bounds)
- GitHub Action workflow for manual validation and adapter refresh

This establishes the source-of-truth control layer for all AI-assisted
development in the repo. All models should reference .ai/skills/project-spec.md
and their model-specific adapter when working on TrustSignal code.

Spec-Version: 1.0

* Fix: Remove unused spawn import, convert absolute paths to relative, sanitize benchmark host/port info

* Fix: Remove orphaned gitlink, sync AI skill validation commands, fix wiki relative paths

- Remove .worktrees/integration-merge orphaned gitlink
- Update project-spec.md, model adapters, and executor-agent with npm run validate/security:audit
- Pin GitHub Action versions to specific commit SHAs
- Fix relative paths for docs, demo, and openapi in wiki documentation

* Security: Resolve high severity vulnerability in pdf2json (@xmldom/xmldom)

* Fix wiki relative paths, nav .md extensions, workflow permissions/node version, script refs

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TrustSignal-dev/TrustSignal/sessions/a517d829-50a8-4a55-8832-0c033444abfb

Co-authored-by: chrismaz11 <24700273+chrismaz11@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Explicitly configure CodeQL languages to bypass failing Swift analysis

A single Swift file in vantademo/public/code4.swift was triggering CodeQL's auto-detection of Swift, which failed due to a lack of Xcode project structure. This change explicitly targets javascript-typescript and python.

- Fig 3 Panel B: carb_treated array corrected for 5/11 patients (c,d,g,h,k were scrambled)

commit.message:
Add KQL entity name sanitization and query/command validation (#149)

Entity name sanitization:
- kql_escape_entity_name(): validates and wraps entity names in ['...']
- Accepts properly escaped (['name'], ["name"]) or plain names
- Rejects names with embedded KQL escape sequences to prevent
  query manipulation (e.g., appending sub-queries via entity names)
- kql_escape_string(): escapes single quotes in KQL string literals
- Applied to all entity name interpolation sites

Query/command endpoint validation:
- kusto_query() rejects management commands (starting with '.')
- kusto_command() rejects KQL queries (not starting with '.')
- Prevents accidentally sending queries to the wrong endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: fix 3 blueprint inconsistencies from rubber-duck review

1. Resource window map: audit blueprint had wrong stage order (HS=496,
   LS=656, CS=816). Correct ISA order is ES=496, HS=656, LS=816 (CS
   shares LS). Fixed to match descriptor_shift.h and Evergreen ISA.

2. Mutable resource capacity: budget check did not subtract UAV tail
   slots (PS: 11, CS: 12) from app-visible capacity. Added
   tail_reserved subtraction to budget_check and audit macros.

3. MUTABLE_BASE off-by-one: shader_args.h and ABI contract
   contradicted (docs said base "moves from 2 to 3"). Clarified:
   MUTABLE_BASE remains 2 (robustness VTX slot), DESCRIPTOR_SHIFT=3
   is where app descriptors begin.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: add HMM degeneracy detection with auto-fallback

After fitting a GaussianHMM, detect collapsed/ghost states using
posterior occupancy and variance pathology. If degenerate, silently
re-fit with fewer components (down to K=1).

Two independent criteria trigger fallback:
1. Extreme sigma ratio (>100:1) between any pair of states
2. Low posterior occupancy (<max(5, 1%*N)) combined with absorbing
   transition (self-transition >0.99)

Exposes fitted_n_states and requested_n_states metadata in the
simulate_hmm_mc return dict for downstream audit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add scan temporal comparison script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: draft report on hypoglycemia from a data-first perspective

Synthesizes findings from ~1,500 experiments across 11 AID patients on
what hypoglycemic events are, why they are harder to predict than
hyperglycemia, and what the counter-regulatory response looks like from
CGM/pump data alone.

Includes:
- 730-line draft report with 15 explicit assumptions for expert review
- 6 generated figures (anatomy of event, prediction ceiling, TIR-TBR
  paradox, nocturnal timing, observable vs unobservable factors)
- Reproducible figure generation script (tools/generate_hypo_report_figures.py)

Key findings documented:
- 2h HYPO AUC 0.860 (deployable) vs overnight ceiling ~0.69
- Counter-regulatory +5.1 mg/dL systematic bias below 70 mg/dL
- AID-induced hypos account for up to 66.9% of events (patient f)
- TIR-TBR paradox: patient k has 95% TIR but 4.87% TBR
- Two recovery phenotypes: active treaters vs passive reliers

commit.message:
perf: single-pass XmlReader parser replacing XmlDocument DOM (#86)

* perf: replace XmlDocument with single-pass XmlReader parser

Replace XmlDocument DOM-based parsing with forward-only XmlReader that
produces pre-parsed ParsedSchema DTOs in a single pass. This eliminates
all XPath queries (SelectNodes), repeated attribute access, and DOM
materialization — which profiling showed consumed ~50% of generator time.

Key changes:
- Add SchemaReader: forward-only XmlReader parser producing ParsedSchema
- Add ParsedSchema: immutable DTO record with Types, Composites, Enums,
  Sets, Messages and root metadata (ByteOrder, Package, Version, etc.)
- Change ICodeGenerator interface from XmlDocument to ParsedSchema
- Refactor all generators to iterate pre-parsed DTOs
- Replace LINQ chains in MessagesCodeGenerator with foreach loops
- Remove dead SchemaParser.cs (superseded by SchemaReader)
- Update profiling app to use new ParsedSchema API
- Add InternalsVisibleTo for profiling project

Profiling results (500 iterations, 5 schemas):
  Before: 2.500 ms/iter | After: 1.193 ms/iter (~52% faster)
  XML Parse: 0.700→0.406ms | Types: 0.760→0.221ms | Messages: 0.640→0.398ms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: cache Cast<T>(), eliminate redundant SumFieldLength, pre-size StringBuilder

- Cache Groups.Cast<GroupDefinition>() and Datas.Cast<DataFieldDefinition>()
  in lazy-initialized typed lists, replacing 24 repeated Cast enumerations
- Remove redundant offset recalculation in AppendFieldsFileContent (offsets
  already set by SumFieldLength in AppendMessageDefinitionConstants)
- Pre-size StringBuilder: 2048+256/field+1024/group+512/data for messages,
  1024 for types/enums/composites/sets
- Replace .Any() with .Count > 0 / HasVariableData property

Profiling (avg of 3 runs, 500 iter × 5 schemas):
  Before: 1.172ms/iter | After: 1.026ms/iter (~12% faster)
  Messages Gen: 0.378→0.359ms | Types Gen: 0.216→0.201ms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace string interpolation with chained Append in code generation

Replace ~150 sb.AppendLine($"...{var}...") calls across all generators
and definition builders with chained sb.AppendTabs(tabs).Append().Append()
.AppendLine() calls. This eliminates intermediate string allocations from
interpolation, reducing GC pressure during code generation.

Added AppendTabs(int) fluent helper to StringBuilderExtensions for
tab-prefixed chained append patterns.

Files changed: MessageDefinition, GroupDefinition, CompositeDefinition,
EnumDefinition, EnumFlagsDefinition, TypeDefinition, all Field definitions,
ValidationGenerator, and semantic type definitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: pre-size Dictionary and List collections to reduce resizing

Pre-size SchemaContext dictionaries (8-32 capacity) and SchemaReader
lists (4-16 capacity) to minimize resize/rehash overhead identified
via profiling (List.AddWithResize ~16%, Dictionary.Resize ~15%).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove profiling trace files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: gitignore profiling trace files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix budget proration and wire up period remaining adjustments

Proration fix:
- Replace daily-rate proration with occurrence-based calculation.
  A biweekly \ item in a 15-day period now shows \ (1
  occurrence) instead of \ (daily rate × days).
- occurrencesInPeriod rounds to nearest whole occurrence, with
  a minimum of 1 per period.

Period remaining fix:
- Checking off a budget item now updates that period's Remaining
  balance, not just the final Free Spending total.
- Period 1 checkoffs also cascade into Period 2's starting balance
  and remaining.

Add DSTPolicy type with three options:

Supports CronJob, DailyJob, WeeklyJob, and MonthlyJob.
Includes comprehensive unit tests and example.

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

* fix: add missing Content-Type headers to JSON/text response endpoints

Add response.setContentType("application/json") and
response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8") before writing JSON to
HTTP responses in 9 action classes. Also fix two instances of
text/javascript → application/json in DocumentPreview2Action,
and add text/plain Content-Type for plain-text ID responses in
CaseManagementEntry2Action and ERefer2Action.

Resolves Semgrep XSS alerts for untyped response outputs.

commit.message:
Remove `models/` intermediate directory from persistent artifact layout (#18)

* Add artifact plugin layout support

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Tyler-R-Kendrick/agentic-metacognition/sessions/c4fc744a-8f56-4aec-9185-8a46859c2858

Co-authored-by: Tyler-R-Kendrick <145080887+Tyler-R-Kendrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* Finish artifact plugin docs and tests

Co-authored-by: Tyler-R-Kendrick <145080887+Tyler-R-Kendrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restructure artifacts to per-feature layout: artifacts/<model>/<feature>/

Remove the intermediate `models/` directory. Each directory under a model
is now an actual feature name (e.g. `chain_of_thought`, `react`) instead
of an arbitrary plugin bundle name. Discovery and runtime persistence
write one directory per feature. Session-level files go at the model
level.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Tyler-R-Kendrick/agentic-metacognition/sessions/275227aa-b799-4ba7-896f-c5c0332a2b4c

Co-authored-by: Tyler-R-Kendrick <145080887+Tyler-R-Kendrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve merge conflicts with main: adopt new plugin API, remove models/ intermediate directory

- Merge origin/main and resolve conflicts in artifact_plugins.py, agent.py, __init__.py
- Take main's new plugin API (write_artifact_plugin, load_model_artifact_bundle, etc.)
- Remove models/ intermediate directory from artifact paths: artifacts/<model>/<plugin>/
- Move gpt2/standard from artifacts/models/gpt2/standard/ to artifacts/gpt2/standard/
- Remove obsolete per-feature directories from old branch
- Update all tests and documentation to use new paths

plan, and TDR to document String fields with application-layer validation

sec: suppress 36 Semgrep CRLF log injection false positives in ManageDocument2Action (#1496)

* Initial plan

* sec: add nosemgrep CRLF log injection suppression to ManageDocument2Action

Add // nosemgrep: crlf-injection-logs-deepsemgrep, crlf-injection-logs
inline comments to all 36 log lines in ManageDocument2Action.java that
use LogSanitizer.sanitize(). These are false positives because
Semgrep's taint analysis does not recognize LogSanitizer.sanitize()
(which calls Encode.forJava() internally) as a CRLF sanitizer.

No code logic changes — all LogSanitizer.sanitize() calls remain intact.

Fixes #1432

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Build complete corporate website for 한성우레탄 (Hansung Urethane Co., Ltd.)

- Next.js 15.5.15 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- KR/EN i18n with next-intl (routing at /ko/ and /en/)
- Hero carousel with Swiper.js (3 slides with fade effect)
- Home page: stats, 4 product cards, 3 core values, contact form, map
- Product detail pages for HS-100, HS-200, HS-300, HS-400
- About pages: overview, history timeline, track record table
- Resources page with download request modal
- Notice board: list + detail pages
- Admin panel: dashboard, hero, products, values, stats, about,
  resources, notices, leads (CSV export), sections, users management
- API routes: inquiry, catalog-download, send-email
- Supabase client/server helpers + migration SQL
- Resend email integration (inquiry + auto-reply)
- Navy (#1B2A6B) + Gold (#D4A843) brand color scheme
- Upgraded to Next.js 15.5.15 and Swiper 12.1.3 for security

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Sungjun-an-ai <263823576+Sungjun-an-ai@users.noreply.github.com>

Add ephemeral spending adjustments and budget item checkoffs

All client-side state (gone on refresh, no backend needed):

- Budget items in each period get a checkbox. Checking one off
  adds its prorated amount back to Free Spending (strikethrough
  styling shows it's been accounted for)
- '+ Adjustment' button at the bottom opens an inline form for
  ad-hoc tweaks (description + positive/negative dollar amount)
- Custom adjustments shown in their own section with remove (x)
- Free Spending total reflects all adjustments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: set init_params='stc' to fully bypass KMeans in GaussianHMM

Pre-setting model.means_ alone is insufficient -- hmmlearn still runs
KMeans when 'm' is in init_params (default 'stmc').  Exclude 'm' so
hmmlearn's _needs_init check returns False and our percentile-based
means are respected without the KMeans code path executing at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
